input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Make application working directory non-writable to prevent staging", "Restrict read permissions on sensitive files to prevent collection", "Deploy forward proxy to filter egress web traffic"], "chosen_mitigation": "Make application working directory non-writable to prevent staging", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 6, "reached_mitigation_limit": false}

{"comment": "The implementation is approved. The command `chmod a-w /usr/src/app` correctly removes write permissions from the application's working directory for all users. Based on the attack report, the adversary needs to write to this directory to create both the 'staged' subdirectory (T1074.001) and the 'staged.tar.gz' archive (T1560.001). By making this directory non-writable, these key steps in the attack chain are effectively blocked. This is a sound security practice and a direct mitigation for the observed techniques.", "approved": true, "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Make application working directory non-writable to prevent staging", "mitigations_counter": 6, "mitigation_iteration": 1, "reached_mitigation_iteration_limit": false}
